mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in 65% of observations during late-cycle phases or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 240 signals entrenched inflation that forces equity market repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 60% of triggered months or the signal predictive power vanishes after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day returns are negative in 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates inventory accumulation rather than genuine demand, leading to earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in 60% of cases or loses significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization that boosts near-term corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 55% of triggered instances during recovery phases or the signal IC drops below 0.02.
